name: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key
description: The system uses a user-controlled key (such as a URL parameter, form field, or cookie) to directly reference data objects without verifying that the authenticated user is authorized to access that specific object. This is commonly known as Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) or Broken Object Level Authorization (BOLA).
detection_pattern: INGRESS node provides a user-controlled identifier (ID, key, filename) that flows directly to a STORAGE node for data retrieval without an AUTH node verifying that the authenticated user owns or is permitted to access the referenced object.
